choices.text: Enables aggressive caching with `Cache-Control: immutable, max-age=31536000` (1 year). Because the URL contains a content hash, content can never change at that URL — only new versions appear at new URLs. Browsers and CDNs can cache the file forever; when you deploy a new build, your HTML references the new hashed filename, browsers fetch the new one, old one gracefully expires. Without hashing, you'd need short TTLs and cache invalidation on every deploy, both worse for performance. The pattern is universal in modern web build tooling — Webpack, Vite, Rollup, esbuild all do this automatically. The HTML itself gets short TTLs (60-300s) because it changes; the hashed assets it references get year-long TTLs. Combined, your users get the fastest possible repeat visits (assets cached) with the freshness needed (HTML updates quickly). Standard performance pattern; one of the highest-leverage decisions in web delivery.

questions.explanation: Versioned URLs let you cache forever (immutable). New deploy = new URL = browsers fetch fresh.

questions.prompt: After deploying a CDN, attackers find your origin IP and bypass the CDN. Defense?

choices.text: Lock down origin firewall to allow only CDN IPs. Every major CDN publishes its IP ranges (Cloudflare's IP list at cloudflare.com/ips/, AWS CloudFront managed prefix list, Fastly's docs). Configure your origin's security group / firewall to ALLOW only those IPs on ports 80/443; deny everything else. Now even if attackers find your origin IP, they can't bypass — direct connections are dropped at the firewall. Pair with: origin pulls from CDN with TLS (the CDN-to-origin link is encrypted), authenticated CDN-to-origin (some CDNs let you set a custom header value that origin requires — `True-Client-IP` or proprietary auth). DNS-level hiding helps too (don't put origin IP in any public DNS record). Without origin lockdown, the CDN's DDoS protection and WAF can be circumvented entirely; an attacker who finds the origin attacks it directly. Standard hardening; one of the first post-CDN setup tasks.

questions.explanation: Origin firewall allows only CDN IPs. CDN publishes its ranges; configure security groups accordingly.
